content: DILI management: (1) Immediately discontinue suspected agent(s); (2) Assess severity (bilirubin, INR, encephalopathy); (3) Rule out other causes (viral hepatitis, autoimmune, ischemic, biliary obstruction); (4) For acetaminophen toxicity, administer N-acetylcysteine within 8-10 hours; (5) Consider corticosteroids for immune-mediated DILI (checkpoint inhibitor hepatitis, DRESS); (6) Monitor LFTs weekly until improvement, then monthly until normalization; (7) Refer to hepatology if bilirubin >3x ULN with ALT elevation (Hy's Law indicates 10% risk of liver failure/transplant); (8) Avoid rechallenge with implicated drug. Most cases resolve within weeks to months after discontinuation. Chronic DILI may develop into vanishing bile duct syndrome or cirrhosis.
